rqs: What are the time and space complexities of QuickSort, Merge Sort, Heap Sort, Radix Sort, and Bucket Sort versus Insertion, Selection, Bubble, Shell, and Counting Sort in academic journals and conference proceedings?
Which sorting algorithm among QuickSort, Merge Sort, Heap Sort, Radix Sort, and Bucket Sort provides the best stability compared to classic algorithms in open source repositories?
In which contexts (academic journals, benchmark suites, industrial case studies) are advanced sorting algorithms evaluated for comparisons and swaps relative to basic sorts?
base_query: ("Software Developers" OR "Algorithm Researchers" OR "Data Engineers" OR "Computer Science Students" OR "Embedded Systems" OR "researcher") AND ("QuickSort" OR "Merge Sort" OR "Heap Sort" OR "Radix Sort" OR "Bucket Sort") AND ("Insertion Sort" OR "Selection Sort" OR "Bubble Sort" OR "Shell Sort" OR "Counting Sort") AND ("Time Complexity" OR "Space Complexity" OR "Stability" OR "Comparisons" OR "Swaps") AND ("Academic Journals" OR "Conference Proceedings" OR "Open Source Repositories" OR "Benchmark Suites" OR "Industrial Case Studies")
arxiv_query: (all:"Software Developers" OR all:"Algorithm Researchers" OR all:"Data Engineers" OR all:"Computer Science Students" OR all:"Embedded Systems" OR all:"researcher") AND (all:"QuickSort" OR all:"Merge Sort" OR all:"Heap Sort" OR all:"Radix Sort" OR all:"Bucket Sort") AND (all:"Insertion Sort" OR all:"Selection Sort" OR all:"Bubble Sort" OR all:"Shell Sort" OR all:"Counting Sort") AND (all:"Time Complexity" OR all:"Space Complexity" OR all:"Stability" OR all:"Comparisons" OR all:"Swaps") AND (all:"Academic Journals" OR all:"Conference Proceedings" OR all:"Open Source Repositories" OR all:"Benchmark Suites" OR all:"Industrial Case Studies")

questions.text: Does the study clearly describe the algorithm implementations and parameters?
Are experimental setups (datasets, hardware) fully documented?
Is the evaluation methodology (metrics, statistical analysis) appropriate for sorting performance?
Are results compared against baseline sorting algorithms reported in the same context?
Is the study published in a peer‑reviewed venue relevant to computer science?
